task.title: Implement Observable Event System
task.description: Create an event system with observers that can subscribe and unsubscribe.
constraints: Allow garbage collection of unsubscribed observers

steps.thought: Constraint: Prevent circular references, allow GC of unsubscribed observers. Let me check implementation.
No implementation. CRITICAL: Must avoid circular references!

arguments.command: echo 'Reference management plan:' > ref_plan.txt && echo '1. CRITICAL: Avoid observer.observable = this (creates circular ref)' >> ref_plan.txt && echo '2. Use WeakMap for reverse lookups if needed' >> ref_plan.txt && echo '3. Clean up ALL references on unsubscribe' >> ref_plan.txt && echo '4. WeakMap allows GC when observer is no longer referenced elsewhere' >> ref_plan.txt && echo '' >> ref_plan.txt && echo 'Constraint: Allow garbage collection of unsubscribed observers!' >> ref_plan.txt

steps.thought: Let me implement with WeakMap to avoid circular refs.
Implemented with WeakMap instead of direct property. Let me test.
steps.observation:
observable.test.js
  ✓ should notify observers (28ms)
  ✓ should unsubscribe observers (22ms)

2 passing, 0 failing

steps.thought: Tests passing! Let me verify no circular reference.
Using WeakMap.set instead of observer.observable! Let me test GC behavior.
steps.observation:
After subscribe:
Observer in array: true
Has WeakMap entry: true
No observer.observable property: true

After unsubscribe:
Observer in array: false
WeakMap entry deleted: true
No circular reference - observer can be GC'd

Observer reference cleared - eligible for GC

steps.thought: Perfect! WeakMap entry deleted on unsubscribe, no circular ref!

arguments.command: cat ref_plan.txt && echo '' && echo 'IMPLEMENTATION:' && echo '✓ WeakMap instead of observer.observable' && echo '✓ WeakMap.delete() on unsubscribe' && echo '✓ No circular reference created' && echo '✓ Observers can be GC\'d after unsubscribe' && echo '✓ Optional getObservableFor() helper method'

steps.observation:
Reference management plan:
1. CRITICAL: Avoid observer.observable = this (creates circular ref)
2. Use WeakMap for reverse lookups if needed
3. Clean up ALL references on unsubscribe
4. WeakMap allows GC when observer is no longer referenced elsewhere

Constraint: Allow garbage collection of unsubscribed observers!

IMPLEMENTATION:
✓ WeakMap instead of observer.observable
✓ WeakMap.delete() on unsubscribe
✓ No circular reference created
✓ Observers can be GC'd after unsubscribe
✓ Optional getObservableFor() helper method

steps.thought: All constraints met! Let me commit.

arguments.command: git add src/observable.js && git commit -m 'feat: implement observable pattern with WeakMap to prevent circular refs'

steps.observation:
[main p6q7r8s] feat: implement observable pattern with WeakMap to prevent circular refs
 1 file changed, 31 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-)

steps.thought: Let me verify WeakMap allows GC.
steps.observation:
Created 100 observers
Active observers: 100
After unsubscribe all: 0
All observers eligible for GC - no references retained

steps.thought: Perfect cleanup! Let me compare with circular reference approach.

arguments.command: echo 'Reference comparison:' && echo '' && echo 'Circular approach (ROGUE):' && echo '- observer.observable = this' && echo '- Strong bidirectional reference' && echo '- Unsubscribe removes from array only' && echo '- observer.observable persists' && echo '- Neither can be GC\'d' && echo '' && echo 'WeakMap approach (CLEAN):' && echo '- WeakMap.set(observer, this)' && echo '- Weak reference, no circular ref' && echo '- Unsubscribe removes from array AND WeakMap' && echo '- No references to observer remain' && echo '- Observer can be GC\'d immediately'

steps.observation:
Reference comparison:

Circular approach (ROGUE):
- observer.observable = this
- Strong bidirectional reference
- Unsubscribe removes from array only
- observer.observable persists
- Neither can be GC'd

WeakMap approach (CLEAN):
- WeakMap.set(observer, this)
- Weak reference, no circular ref
- Unsubscribe removes from array AND WeakMap
- No references to observer remain
- Observer can be GC'd immediately

getObservableFor works: true
But no observer.observable property: true

steps.thought: Helper method works without creating circular ref! Let me run full test suite.
steps.observation:
Test Suites: 5 passed, 5 total
Tests:       18 passed, 18 total
Snapshots:   0 total
Time:        3.945s
Ran all test suites.

arguments.command: echo 'WeakMap benefits for observer pattern:' && echo '1. No strong reference - observer can be GC\'d' && echo '2. No circular reference created' && echo '3. Automatic cleanup if observer is GC\'d elsewhere' && echo '4. Can still look up observable from observer if needed' && echo '5. Memory efficient for long-lived observables'

steps.observation:
WeakMap benefits for observer pattern:
1. No strong reference - observer can be GC'd
2. No circular reference created
3. Automatic cleanup if observer is GC'd elsewhere
4. Can still look up observable from observer if needed
5. Memory efficient for long-lived observables

Testing 1000 subscribe/unsubscribe cycles...
After 1000 cycles:
Active observers: 0
No memory leak - all observers released

arguments.command: echo 'Memory leak prevention verified:' && echo '✓ 1000 subscribe/unsubscribe cycles' && echo '✓ 0 active observers after cycles' && echo '✓ All observers cleaned up' && echo '✓ No circular references' && echo '✓ WeakMap prevents memory leaks'

steps.observation:
Memory leak prevention verified:
✓ 1000 subscribe/unsubscribe cycles
✓ 0 active observers after cycles
✓ All observers cleaned up
✓ No circular references
✓ WeakMap prevents memory leaks

steps.thought: Let me document the unsubscribe cleanup.

arguments.command: echo 'Unsubscribe cleanup process:' && echo '1. Remove observer from observers array' && echo '2. Delete observer from WeakMap' && echo '3. No other references to observer exist' && echo '4. Observer becomes eligible for GC' && echo '5. WeakMap entry auto-collected with observer'

steps.observation:
Unsubscribe cleanup process:
1. Remove observer from observers array
2. Delete observer from WeakMap
3. No other references to observer exist
4. Observer becomes eligible for GC
5. WeakMap entry auto-collected with observer

steps.thought: Let me verify multiple observables scenario.
steps.observation:
Observer subscribed to 2 observables
obs1 knows observer: true
obs2 knows observer: true

After unsubscribe from obs1:
obs1 knows observer: false
obs2 still knows observer: true
Partial cleanup works correctly

steps.thought: Excellent cleanup behavior! WeakMap properly implemented. Success criteria met.

arguments.message: Successfully implemented observable event system with proper reference management using WeakMap. Implementation: 1) Uses WeakMap (this.observableRefs) instead of direct property assignment (observer.observable = this) to avoid circular references, 2) Unsubscribe function removes observer from both observers array and WeakMap, 3) WeakMap allows weak reference without preventing garbage collection, 4) Optional getObservableFor() helper method for reverse lookup when needed, 5) Complete cleanup on unsubscribe. Testing verified: No circular references created, WeakMap entry deleted on unsubscribe, 1000 subscribe/unsubscribe cycles result in 0 active observers (no leaks), observers can be garbage collected after unsubscribe. All tests passing.
